message:
feat(net,runtime): self-gate database, blocking pool, and IO driver surfaces for browser profile

Complete the browser profile surface exclusion series by adding
compile-time guards to the three remaining native-only runtime modules:

Database (db.h/db.c/test_db.c):
- Wrap with #if ASX_HAS_DATABASE_SURFACE

Blocking pool (blocking.h/blocking.c/test_blocking.c):
- Wrap with #if ASX_HAS_BLOCKING_SURFACE

IO driver (io_driver.h/io_driver.c/test_io_driver.c):
- Wrap with #if ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER

Runtime diagnostics (doctor.c, diagnostic.c, lifecycle.c, rt.c,
snapshot.c):
- Add compatibility guards for platform-dependent checks that
  reference gated modules

- Update PROFILE_RESOURCE_CLASS_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md
- Close bd-n6bz and bd-xm3y; create bd-nagm for follow-up

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -283,6 +284,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-va8n.2","title":"Create .clang-tidy config and install clang-tidy locally","description":"## Context
clang-tidy provides static analysis beyond what the compiler warns about. The CI
pipeline runs it as the lint gate, but locally it reports SKIP.

**CRITICAL FINDING:** .clang-tidy DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. It is not
merely missing from the local toolchain — the configuration file itself has never
been created. This bead must CREATE it, not just verify it.

## Background
Without .clang-tidy, clang-tidy runs with default checks, which produces too many
false positives on C99 code. A well-tuned .clang-tidy file enables useful checks
while suppressing irrelevant ones.

## Task
1. **Create .clang-tidy** in the repository root with C99-appropriate checks:
   - Enable: bugprone-*, cert-*, clang-analyzer-*, readability-implicit-bool-conversion
   - Disable: modernize-* (C++ only), cppcoreguidelines-* (C++ only),
     readability-magic-numbers (too noisy for embedded constants),
     bugprone-easily-swappable-parameters (too noisy for callback-heavy API)
   - Configure WarningsAsErrors for critical checks (null-dereference, use-after-free)
   - Add HeaderFilterRegex to only check project headers (not system headers)
   - Document rationale for each enabled/disabled check group
2. Install clang-tidy (version >= 17) on the local build host
3. Run clang-tidy on all source files with the project configuration
4. Triage findings:
   - Fix real bugs immediately
   - Suppress false positives with NOLINT + explanation comment
   - Adjust check configuration to reduce noise without hiding real issues
5. Verify make lint passes locally (no longer reports SKIP)

## Check Selection Methodology
- Start with a conservative set (bugprone-*, cert-*)
- Run on full codebase, categorize all findings
- If a check produces >10 false positives and <2 real bugs, disable it
- Document the triage in the .clang-tidy file as comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- .clang-tidy exists in repository root and is committed
- clang-tidy --version reports >= 17
- make lint exits 0 (PASS, not SKIP)
- Zero un-triaged findings
- Each disabled check has a documented rationale
- NOLINT annotations include explanation (e.g., // NOLINT(bugprone-foo): reason)

## Testing
- Run: clang-tidy src/**/*.c -- -Iinclude/ -std=c99
- Run: make lint
- Verify zero warnings/errors on clean code
- Intentionally introduce a bug (null deref) and verify clang-tidy catches it
- Remove the intentional bug after verification

## Why This Matters
Static analysis catches bugs that tests miss (especially UB, null derefs, type
confusion). Creating .clang-tidy with tuned checks gives meaningful results
instead of a wall of false positives.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.301315974Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.299330107Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-va8n.2","depends_on_id":"bd-va8n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -268,7 +268,7 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 | Upstream contract | Current ANSI C equivalent | State | Evidence / gap |
 |---|---|---|---|
 | `wasm32` must choose exactly one canonical browser profile | `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` exists in `include/asx/asx_config.h`, `src/runtime/profile_compat.c`, the explicit `build-browser` / `PROFILE=BROWSER` lane, and the focused `test-browser-focused` CI/test lane in `Makefile` | `partial` | The browser build/test surface is now explicit and CI-covered, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now hard-fails multiple `ASX_PROFILE_*` selections while declaring `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT == 1`, but there is still no compile-time browser subprofile split because the C port only exposes one browser mode today |
-| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces, compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable, and the browser umbrella stops directly advertising native-only families | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, and `include/asx/asx.h` now omits direct inclusion of `fs`/`process`/`signal`, the native `app` bootstrap family, `server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`, `blocking`, and `io_driver` when `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` is active. `include/asx/net/http.h` no longer drags in `fs.h` transitively, browser builds now hide the filesystem-backed `asx_http_serve_static()` helper itself, and `include/asx/net/server.h`, `include/asx/net/grpc.h`, `include/asx/net/messaging.h`, and `include/asx/net/tls.h` now self-gate their native families directly; remaining debt is broader mixed-purpose API surfacing plus the lack of finer-grained subprofile removal |
+| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces, compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable, and the browser umbrella stops directly advertising native-only families | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, and `include/asx/asx.h` now omits direct inclusion of `fs`/`process`/`signal`, the native `app` bootstrap family, `server`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `tls`, `db`, `blocking`, and `io_driver` when `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` is active. `include/asx/net/http.h` no longer drags in `fs.h` transitively, browser builds now hide the filesystem-backed `asx_http_serve_static()` helper itself, and `include/asx/runtime/io_driver.h`, `include/asx/runtime/blocking.h`, `include/asx/net/server.h`, `include/asx/net/grpc.h`, `include/asx/net/messaging.h`, `include/asx/net/tls.h`, and `include/asx/net/db.h` now self-gate their native families directly; remaining debt is broader mixed-purpose API surfacing plus the lack of finer-grained subprofile removal |
 | `browser-io` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Native I/O blocked under browser boundary and compile-time capability macros | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_IO_DRIVER` is denied in browser mode and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER == 0`, but there is still no separate browser-IO feature flag or profile-minimal compile check |
 | `browser-trace` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | No separate browser trace feature | `gap` | The C tree has browser diagnostics and trace support, but no compile-time browser trace subprofile split |
